description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and angular form, featuring a mix of sharp edges and smoother, weathered surfaces. The coloration is predominantly a blend of light gray, off-white, and muted brown, with some darker patches and subtle hints of reddish tones, especially visible in the crevices. One side of the rock displays a prominent flat face, while another side reveals deep cavities and rough, pitted textures, suggesting natural erosion or breakage. The surface is generally coarse and matte, with visible grain and unevenness typical of sedimentary or metamorphic stone. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible inclusions of other materials or artificial modifications. Its size and mass would make it a significant obstacle or barrier in an urban environment, suitable for use as a naturalistic landscaping element or boundary marker.
description: A large, rugged, angular rock with a mix of light gray, off-white, and brown tones, featuring rough, pitted surfaces and deep cavities. Its solid, weathered appearance makes it a substantial natural barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view highlights the elongated, angular profile of the rock, showing a prominent ridge and a mix of light and dark surface tones.
description_view_1: The rock appears broad and flat from this angle, with a smoother, weathered face and subtle color variations.
description_view_2: This side reveals deep cavities and rough, pitted textures, with visible reddish-brown patches and a more irregular surface.
description_view_3: A rounded, bulky aspect of the rock is visible here, with a generally coarse, matte surface and muted gray coloration.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside barrier
